articles.title: Man seeks answers after mystery object appears to fall from sky and crash into car on Hwy. 427
articles.summary: An 18-year-old driver had an encounter with a UFO on Highway 427 earlier this month. In this case, though, UFO stands for Unidentified ‘Falling’ Object. Ayden Koo was driving in the northbound lanes of the busy highway near Finch Avenue on July 11 at around 5:30 p.m. when an object appeared to fall from the […]

articles.title: Family of Barrie-area teens shot by police launch $17.5M lawsuit
articles.summary: The family of two teens shot by police in the summer of 2024 has launched a $17.5-million lawsuit against the South Simcoe Police Service, four of its officers, and the Ontario Provincial Police. Police were called to a home on Shoreview Drive in Innisfil, east of Barrie, on Aug. 15, 2024, following reports of a […]

articles.title: Brampton couple wins $70M Lotto Max jackpot after Mother’s Day gift turns into ‘the surprise of a lifetime’
articles.summary: A Brampton couple who came to Canada from Jamaica “with nothing” are now celebrating a life‑changing $70‑million Lotto Max jackpot, all thanks to a Mother’s Day gift. Audrey and Wills W., longtime lottery players, won the massive prize in the May 8 draw. The ticket — a Lotto Max Quick Pick — was a simple […]

articles.title: Police seek suspects after jewellery robbery on TTC subway at Warden Station
articles.summary: Toronto police are asking for the public’s help identifying two men accused of robbing a passenger and threatening him with a knife on the subway last month. Officers were called around 7 p.m. on June 6 to Warden Station in the Warden Avenue and St. Clair Avenue East area after reports of a robbery involving […]

articles.title: Youth sexually assaulted on TTC bus, police say; suspect images released
articles.summary: Toronto police are asking for the public’s help identifying a man accused of sexually assaulting a youth on a TTC bus in the city’s east end last week. Officers were called around 4:05 p.m. on July 16 to the Victoria Park Avenue and Danforth Avenue area after reports of a sexual assault involving a young […]

articles.title: Two alleged MS‑13 gang members charged in Peel human trafficking investigation
articles.summary: Peel Regional Police (PRP) say two men with alleged ties to the street gang MS‑13 (Mara Salvatrucha) have been arrested and charged in a human trafficking investigation that began last year. Investigators, working alongside the Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) and the Correctional Service of Canada (CSC), executed four search warrants in Mississauga and the Collingwood […]

articles.title: Fire at Scarborough commercial plaza being investigated as suspicious, authorities say
articles.summary: What started as a vehicle fire spread to a commercial plaza near Eglinton Avenue E. and Kennedy Road early Wednesday morning, Toronto Fire says.

articles.title: How wildfire smoke travels from Ontario to New York City — and turns distant city skies orange
articles.summary: Wildfires that have torched huge areas of forest and devastated communities are also sending smoke far and wide, prompting air quality warnings in distant cities and heated political rhetoric from the U.S. Experts say there's nothing particularly unusual about this summer's smoke patterns — but they say wildfires are getting worse, and that's not likely to stop on either side of the border.

articles.title: Woman, 18, charged with attempted murder after teen stabbed on bus: Peel police
articles.summary: An 18-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder after a teen was stabbed on board a transit bus in Mississauga, Ont, police say.

articles.title: Toronto's Pearson airport launching 2nd phase of multi-billion dollar construction project
articles.summary: Officials at Toronto’s Pearson International Airport launched the second phase of a multi-billion dollar construction project aimed at modernizing and expanding the facility on Tuesday.

articles.title: Toronto step closer to 'much needed' Liberty Village traffic and community additions
articles.summary: The City of Toronto has voted on plans to update Liberty Village's infrastructure, including a new community center, Canada Post service and school development.
